fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 14-year-old brought by coach after expressing that 'everyone would be better off' without them following being cut from team.
fields.Subjective: Patient is a 14-year-old who uses they/them pronouns presenting after telling basketball coach that everyone would be better off without them after being cut from varsity team. Patient reports passive thoughts over past two days of wishing they did not exist but denies plan, method, or intent. Describes feeling like a failure and disappointment. Basketball has been central to identity. Sleep disrupted, appetite decreased. Has supportive friends on JV team. Close with mother and older sibling. Currently no medications. School performance good. No prior mental health concerns. (Social Worker Marcus Brown, LCSW)

Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Passive suicidal ideation without plan or intent; athletic identity loss

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Adjustment disorder with depressed mood related to athletic disappointment; passive suicidal ideation.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Cut from varsity basketball team; athletic identity; friends on JV team; supportive family; good academic performance; no prior mental health issues.
Assessment_and_Plan: Low risk: passive SI without plan or intent in context of athletic setback.
Protective factors: family support, JV team friends, academic success, coach concern.
Normal reaction to significant disappointment and identity challenge.
Brief counseling for resilience and identity beyond athletics.
Reframe JV as opportunity for growth and leadership.
Explore other interests to diversify identity.
Coach to provide supportive feedback and growth opportunities.
Safety plan with mother and coach.
Follow-up with counselor within one week.
Safe for discharge with appropriate support.
fields.Author: Marcus Brown, LCSW (Social Worker)
